title: MITRE 2018/2019 & 2025 Biennial Review Reports

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: MITRE 2018/2019 & 2025 Biennial Review Reports
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

In a letter dated August 27, 2018, the U.S. House of Representatives Committee on Energy and Commerce send a letter to MITRE regarding the CVE program [1]. In it, the house committee recommended that MITRE perform biennial reviews of the CVE program [2] to be submitted . I am requesting a copy of these reviews for 2018 or the first year they were submitted, as well as copies of the reports submitted in 2025. These were to be submitted to the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) and the managing agency of the CVE program, which is the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA).

[1] https://embed.documentcloud.org/documents/4788035-082718-MITRE-Recommendations-for-CVE-Program-1/
[2] https://www.cve.org/

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brian Martin

title: California Governor's Office of Business and Economic Development: Administrative Correspondence Regarding Atomic Machines, Inc.

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: California Governor's Office of Business and Economic Development: Administrative Correspondence Regarding Atomic Machines, Inc.
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting the following public records held by the Governor's Office of Business and Economic Development (GO-Biz):

Records Requested:
All correspondence, including but not limited to emails, email attachments, calendar invitations, meeting minutes, and text messages, between any staff members of GO-Biz and the following representatives of Atomic Machines, Inc.: 

Jeff Holden (CEO)
Klaus Zietlow (CTO)
Anjana Balakumar (Finance)
Tylee Holden (Growth)
Stephen Drinan (Go-to-Market)
Brandon Connors or Kevin Mukai (Manufacturing/Operations)
Erina DuBois (Executive Assistant to the CEO)

Search Parameters:

Date Range: January 1, 2024, to March 16th 2026

Please include a search for any emails sent to or from the @atomicmachines.com domain.

Keywords: Please include (but do not limit the search to) records containing the following terms: "incentives," "site selection," "expansion," "hiring commitments," "milestones,", "Jeff Holden", "Holden," "Atomic," or "Emeryville."

Legal Context & Segregability:
I am aware of the previous response from your office (dated March 16, 2026) citing Revenue and Taxation Code § 19542. Please note that this request is not seeking tax returns or proprietary financial data required to be filed for tax purposes. Rather, it seeks administrative communications regarding the coordination and status of the project.

Pursuant to Government Code § 7922.525, if any portion of these records is deemed exempt under RTC § 19542 or any other statute, you are required to redact the exempt information and provide the "reasonably segregable portion" of the records. A blanket denial of all correspondence based on a tax-return exemption is inconsistent with the transparency requirements of the CPRA. If certain parts of this request or certain portions must be redacted please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

Considering the implications of this technology, and the danger in hiding the financing and support of these types of companies from the public, there is a public interest in the presumption of transparency. Thank you, and I look forward to working together in resolving this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request records related to the University of Nevada, Las Vegas decision to remain in the Mountain West Conference and the agreement regarding the Mountain West men’s and women’s basketball championships being held at the Thomas & Mack Center.
I request the following records created between January 1, 2023 and December 31, 2024:
1. Emails, text messages, or other communications discussing the location of the Mountain West basketball championships, including references to “Thomas & Mack Center,” “Las Vegas host agreement,” or “conference tournament location.”
2. The documents of understanding or other agreements between UNLV and the Mountain West Conference related to conference membership through 2032.
3. Documents, reports, or presentations related to financial incentives or guarantees associated with hosting the Mountain West basketball championships in Las Vegas.
4. Communications between UNLV leadership and Mountain West Conference officials regarding tournament hosting arrangements.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

title: Internal Complaints (2010 - Present) (Sheriff)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

For the period from January 1, 2010 to the date this request was filed, please provide a spreadsheet of all complaints against officers in CSV or Excel format with only the following columns: Investigation/Complaint No., Incident Type, Occurred Date, Complaint Reported On Date, Employee Badge Number, Employee First Name, Employee Last Name, Allegation, Allegation: Policy/Directive Violated, Allegation: Finding, Disposition, Discipline Imposed.

Please provide all complaints, regardless of severity,in cluding any complaints that may not have been classified for full investigation and were referred to an employee's supervisor for counseling or retraining as well as complaints that were investigated by an employee's command instead of the normal internal affairs process. I would ask that you fulfill my request in a single installment in one spreadsheet instead of splitting it up into multiple annual spreadsheets if possible.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

title: UConn Spending on Holiday Decorations and Related Electricity Costs

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: UConn Spending on Holiday Decorations and Related Electricity Costs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

For the period November 23 through January 1 for each year from 2021-2025, across all University of Connecticut campuses:
1. Any and all records, invoices, purchase orders, receipts, memos, and reports related to the purchase of holiday decorations and holiday lighting.
2. Any and all records, invoices, receipts, memos, emails, correspondence,  and reports related to the cost of holiday lighting installation. 
3. Any and all records, invoices, purchase orders, receipts, memos, emails, correspondence, and reports related to estimated electricity costs associated with illuminating holiday lighting.
4. Any and all records, invoices, memos, emails, correspondence, and reports related to the daily operating hours for holiday lighting, including how long lighting is illuminated each day.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Julianna D'Addona

title: Mowing & Litter Lettings

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Mowing & Litter Lettings
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, Tenn. Code Ann. § 10-7-503 et seq., I hereby request access to and copies of the following public records related to the TDOT November 7, 2025 Contract Award Letting for Mowing and Litter Removal contracts.

REQUEST 1: Engineer's Estimate Methodology for Mowing and Litter Removal Contracts
The November 7, 2025 letting included multiple mowing and litter removal contracts (Calls 003 through 034) for which TDOT published Engineer's Estimates. I request the following:
a.	Any formula, model, worksheet, spreadsheet, algorithm, or standard operating procedure used by TDOT to generate the Engineer's Estimate for mowing and litter removal contracts, including but not limited to vegetation management contracts let on a per-route or per-zone basis;


REQUEST 2: Rejected Bid Calls – November 7, 2025 Letting
The following four mowing and litter removal contract calls from the November 7, 2025 letting were listed as "ALL BIDS REJECTED":
•	Call 006 – Contract CNZ702 – Mowing and Litter Removal on I-40 – Benton, Carroll, etc. Counties
•	Call 019 – Contract CNZ713 – Mowing and Litter Removal on Various State Routes – Davidson County
•	Call 023 – Contract CNZ717 – Mowing and Litter Removal on I-40 – Fayette, Haywood, etc. Counties
•	Call 033 – Contract CNZ727 – Mowing and Litter Removal on Various State Routes – Shelby County
For each of the four rejected calls above, I request:
a.	TDOT's intended plan of action for maintaining the affected routes during the period in which no contract is in place, including whether the work will be performed by TDOT in-house crews, assigned to an adjacent contract holder, let on an emergency or sole-source basis, or re-let in a subsequent regular letting – and any records documenting that determination.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or USB if not.

Sincerely,

Drek Hawkins

title: Obama Presidential Library: Ustream Social Stream Chat Logs for April 13, 2010 Ben Rhodes Nuclear Security Summit Livestream

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: Ustream Social Stream Chat Logs for April 13, 2010 Ben Rhodes Nuclear Security Summit Livestream
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Respectfully, I request access to records related to the White House livestream titled “Nuclear Security Summit: Taking Your Questions,” featuring Deputy National Security Advisor Ben Rhodes, which occurred on or about April 13, 2010 and was streamed via the White House Ustream channel (ustream.tv/whitehouse).

This Livestream is discussed here:

https://obamawhitehouse.archives.gov/nuclear-security-summit

Specifically, I request the following categories of records created or received between April 10, 2010 and April 20, 2010:

1. Textual logs or transcripts of viewer communications submitted via the Ustream “Social Stream” or live chat interface associated with the livestream event.

2. Any exported datasets, text files, moderation logs, or analytics reports containing viewer chat messages or aggregated social media messages submitted during the livestream.

3. Internal emails or memoranda discussing the collection, moderation, filtering, or selection of viewer questions submitted via the Ustream chat or Social Stream interface for the event.

4. Lists, spreadsheets, or documents compiling viewer-submitted questions derived from the livestream chat or social media aggregation tools used during the event.

5. Technical records relating to the operation of the Ustream livestream during the event, including any reports or summaries generated after the broadcast describing audience engagement or chat activity.

To assist in locating responsive records, the following White House offices and custodians may have been involved in the planning or operation of the livestream:

• Office of New Media / Office of Digital Strategy
• National Security Council staff
• White House video production staff

Likely custodians include:

• Macon Phillips (Director of New Media)
• Jesse Lee (Director of Online Programs)
• Kori Schulman (Digital Strategy / Engagement)
• Katie Stanton (Director of Citizen Participation)
• Andrew Bleeker (Director of Video)
• Ben Rhodes (National Security Council)

The livestream referenced above is documented in contemporaneous White House materials and Internet Archive captures of the White House Ustream channel, including:

https://web.archive.org/web/20100413080516/http://www.ustream.tv/whitehouse

If chat logs or Social Stream data were maintained by the White House or exported from the Ustream platform for internal use, I request copies of those records in their native electronic format if available.

Please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

Thank you for the Obama Presidential Library's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Obama Presidential Library: Records Concerning Analysis of Russian Data Localization Law and Implications for Chinese Cyber Policy

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: Records Concerning Analysis of Russian Data Localization Law and Implications for Chinese Cyber Policy
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request access to records created or received by officials within the National Security Council and related Executive Office of the President components during the administration of Barack Obama concerning analysis of Russia’s data localization regime and its potential implications for internet governance policies in other countries, particularly the People’s Republic of China.

Time Frame: September 1st 2014 to January 20th 2017

This timeframe captures the period following the passage and implementation of Russia’s data localization law and the subsequent blocking of the professional networking platform LinkedIn by the Russian government in 2016.

Offices Likely to Hold Responsive Records

Please search, at minimum, the files of the following components and staff:

National Security Council (NSC) Directorate for East Asian Affairs
National Security Council Directorate for Russia / Eurasia
NSC Directorate for Cybersecurity Coordination
NSC Directorate for International Economics
Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP)
Office of the U.S. Trade Representative (records maintained within EOP systems)

Key personnel whose files may contain responsive material includes, but is not limited to:

Ben Rhodes (likely email address of: Rhodes@nss.eop.gov and/or Rhodes@nsc.eop.gov and/or brhodes@nsc.eop.gov)
Michael Daniel (likely email address of: mdaniel@nsc.eop.gov)
Daniel Russel (likely email address of: RusselDR@state.gov)
Rob Malley (if cyber-policy discussions intersected broader strategy memos) - (likely email address of: rmalley@nsc.eop.gov or rmalley@nss.eop.gov)

Description of Requested Records

I request copies of memoranda, briefing papers, information papers, strategy papers, talking points, email correspondence, or policy analyses that:

Discuss or analyze Russia’s data localization law, specifically Federal Law No. 242-FZ.
Reference the Russian government’s decision to block LinkedIn within the Russian Federation in November 2016.
Evaluate whether Russia’s implementation of data localization or internet blocking policies could serve as a precedent, model, or “blueprint” for other governments seeking to assert digital sovereignty.
Compare Russian internet governance measures with Chinese cyber regulatory frameworks, including:

Cybersecurity Law of the People's Republic of China
China’s Multi-Level Protection Scheme (MLPS)
Broader Chinese concepts of “cyber sovereignty" or "digital sovereignty."

Assess whether Russia’s actions might contribute to:
fragmentation of the global internet
expansion of national data localization regimes
the emergence of a “splinternet.”

Search Terms

To assist archival searches, responsive records may include the following terms:

“LinkedIn Russia block”

“242-FZ”

“data localization Russia”

“precedent for China”

“cyber sovereignty”

"Russian digital sovereignty"

"Chinese digital sovereignty"

“MLPS” or “Multi-Level Protection Scheme”

“splinternet”

“digital trade barriers”

Types of Documents Requested

Please include, but do not limit searches to:

NSC Information Papers
NSC Decision Memos or Deputies Committee briefing materials
interagency policy coordination emails
internal talking points prepared for senior officials

Historical Significance

This request seeks to illuminate how the administration of Barack Obama evaluated the geopolitical implications of emerging digital sovereignty regimes following the events surrounding the Annexation of Crimea by the Russian Federation. In particular, it seeks records reflecting whether policymakers believed Russia’s enforcement actions against foreign internet companies would encourage similar policies in China and other states.

Please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

Thank you for the Obama Library's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Obama Presidential Library: U.S. Government/LinkedIn Correspondence Regarding Russian Law 242-FZ (2015-2017)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: U.S. Government/LinkedIn Correspondence Regarding Russian Law 242-FZ (2015-2017)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Access to and copies of all records maintained by the Barack Obama Presidential Library related to correspondence between the U.S. Government and LinkedIn Corporation concerning Russian Federal Law No. 242-FZ (Data Localization Law).

I. Scope of Search
The timeframe for this request from September 1st, 2015 until noon on January 20th, 2017. This request includes, but is not limited to, email correspondence, audio recordings, video recordings, PowerPoint Presentations, briefing memos, meeting minutes, and diplomatic cables.

II. Targeted Offices & Agencies
Please search the following record groups within the Obama Administration:

National Security Council (NSC): Specifically the Directorate for Russia and Central Asia and the Directorate for Cyber Policy.

Department of Commerce: Specifically the Office of the Secretary and the International Trade Administration (ITA) (Office of Digital Services Industries).

Office of the United States Trade Representative (USTR).

III. Targeted Domains and Entities
I request that a search be conducted for correspondence involving the following email domains and specific high-level executives:

Domains: @linkedin.com, @microsoft.com (post-June 2016).

LinkedIn Executives:

Jeff Weiner (CEO) 
(Requested search against the following likely email addresses: jweiner@linkedin.com, jeff.weiner@linkedin.com, jeffweiner@linkedin.com, j.weiner@linkedin.com)

Erika Rottenberg (General Counsel/VP)
(Requested search against the following likely email addresses: erottenberg@linkedin.com, erika.rottenberg@linkedin.com, erottenberg@linkedin.com, erika_rottenberg@linkedin.com)

Pablo Chavez (VP, Global Public Policy and Government Affairs)
(Requested search against the following likely email addresses: pchavez@linkedin.com, pablo.chavez@linkedin.com, pabloc@linkedin.com, p.chavez@linkedin.com)

Nicole Wong (Former Deputy U.S. CTO with known ties to LinkedIn policy)
(Requested search against the following likely email addresses: nicolew@linkedin.com, Nicole.wong@linkedin.com, nicole_wong@linkedin.com, nicoleawong@linkedin.com, nwong@linkedin.com)

Microsoft Executives:

Brad Smith (President and Chief Legal Office)
(Requested search against the following likely email addresses: brad.smith@microsoft.com, bsmith@microsoft.com, brads@microsoft.com)

John Frank (in late 2016 was Microsoft's VP of EU Government Affairs; often handled "Export" policy issues)
(Request search against the following likely email addresses: john.frank@microsoft.com, jfrank@microsoft.com, johnf@microsoft.com)

Dominic Carr (Microsoft's Head of Public Affairs in late 2016)
Request search against the following likely email addresses: dominic.carr@microsoft.com, dcarr@microsoft.com, domc@microsoft.com)


IV. Keywords & Search Terms
To facilitate a thorough search, please utilize the following keywords:

"LinkedIn", "Roskomnadzor", "242-FZ", "Data Localization", "Moscow City Court", "Tagansky District", "Severance", "Digital Trade Barrier", "Safe Harbor", "Privacy Shield", "Data Sovereignty", "IP Blocking".


This request seeks to clarify the "technical advice" and "compliance pathways" suggested by the U.S. government to private tech entities facing foreign regulatory pressure. It is of significant public interest to understand whether the Obama administration encouraged a policy of non-compliance as a form of digital protest or if technical workarounds (such as "mirroring" or "sharding" data) were deemed technically unfeasible or legally insufficient under Russian law.

Please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

Thank you for the Obama Library's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware
